description: Optimize test suite from 813 to ~220 core tests to eliminate memory spikes and improve dev experience

userStories.title: Delete Low-Value Test Files
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to remove redundant test files so that running the test suite doesn't cause memory spikes to 100%.
userStories.notes: These files test infrastructure, duplicates, and non-critical features. Deleting them removes ~400 tests with minimal risk to user-facing functionality.
implementationNotes: Delete redundant phase test files: test_phase1_phase2.py (47 tests), test_phase1_task3.py (46 tests), test_tui_enhancements.py (27 tests), test_analysis_multifile.py (14 tests)
Delete 5 of 6 networking files: test_networking_rpc.py (32), test_networking_transport.py (28), test_networking_payload.py (22), test_networking_resilience.py (26). Keep test_networking_connection.py

Delete 10 of 11 extension files: test_extension_sandbox.py (25), registry (20), manifest (19), protocol (19), discovery (17), contracts (13), integration (12), cli_command (11), tool (8), manager (6). Keep test_extension_lifecycle.py
Total files to delete: 20 files, ~400 tests removed
After deletion: Run 'python -m pytest --collect-only' to verify remaining tests count (~220)
Verify collection time dropped to <0.5s
acceptanceCriteria: All 20 identified low-value test files successfully deleted
No import errors or broken test references

userStories.title: Consolidate and Reduce Core Test Files
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to reduce redundant test cases in core files so that test suite runs faster and uses less memory.
userStories.notes: Reduce test counts in high-volume files by keeping core flows and removing edge case variations. Maintain coverage of critical paths.
implementationNotes: In test_phase5_tui.py: Reduce from 66 to 20 tests (keep main UI rendering, input handling; remove style/color variations)
In test_phase6_agent.py: Reduce from 54 to 25 tests (keep decision logic, tool calling; remove minor parameter variations)
In test_phase7_config.py: Reduce from 44 to 10 tests (keep config file loading, env override; remove edge cases)
In test_phase8_platform.py: Reduce from 47 to 15 tests (keep platform detection, path handling; remove OS-specific edge cases)
In test_phase9_validation.py: Reduce from 31 to 10 tests (keep core validators; remove format string tests)
Strategy: For each file, identify core test class/function and keep only main path + 1-2 critical edge cases
Remove tests by commenting them out or deleting entire test classes/functions
Run 'pytest tests/test_phaseX.py' for each file to verify remaining tests pass
acceptanceCriteria: All remaining tests in these files pass
Core functionality tests kept: config loading, agent decision logic, TUI main flows
pytest collection time for these files <200ms
ruff check passes on modified files

userStories.title: Verify Cleanup and Validate Coverage
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to verify the test cleanup maintained critical coverage and didn't break functionality.
userStories.notes: Final validation that the cleanup was successful and didn't remove critical tests.
implementationNotes: Run 'pytest --collect-only' and verify total test count is 200-300 (from original 813)
Run full test suite: 'pytest tests/' and verify all remaining tests pass
Check collection time: should be <0.5s (from 1.07s)
Run specific critical tests: march-cli help, mode switching, [[PLAN]] prefix detection
Verify no import errors or broken references in test files
Check that test_phase1_foundation.py, test_phase2_cli.py, test_phase3_github.py all still pass
Verify CLI entry point tests and core user-facing tests remain
acceptanceCriteria: pytest collects 220-300 total tests (minimum 60% reduction from 813)
All remaining tests pass without failures
Critical tests present: mode switching, plan mode, CLI commands, GitHub integration
No import errors or broken test modules
pytest execution time <30s for full suite
